projectName: Web Research Best Practices for research.md
question: How should research.md enforce web research best practices — source triangulation, disconfirmation search, and source quality grading — as process gates, so agents cannot skip rigorous evidence gathering the way they currently cannot skip peer review or criteria locking?
context: research.md currently enforces downstream process gates (criteria locking, peer review, TBD resolution) but is completely silent on how evidence is gathered upstream. An agent can mark a finding CONFIRMED from a single blog post with no disconfirming search. The evidence grading field (UNVERIFIED/REASONED/CONFIRMED) is self-assessed with no structural enforcement. This was discovered during the Tipsy Trailer strategy project where web research was done ad-hoc with no methodology. The DESIGN.md philosophy is 'make the right thing automatic and the wrong thing impossible' — but this principle is not yet applied to evidence gathering.
